content: I know if you copy her own strike/defend it does, I don't think it does for others though it just does whatever the others do(like increased tentacle dmg, str scaling and so on depending on the chars), could be wrong though cause I rarely ever play those over wings/pinions copies and I also don't have E3 on my own tawil

I actually was wondering about it too cause it feels like what is written did not match what happens. So let's say I have 4/4 tentacles. I create another tentacle - the limit is exceeded and they all attack 3 times? Now I have 5/5 tentacles. So if I create another next turn, limit is exceeded again? Thay way it feels like they should multiply way faster than they actually do
I have E2 as well, I only ever play her attack or defend if it’s copied by her skill and therefore at 0 cost

if you create a tentacle at base cap, let's say 4/4, you gain a slot to become 4/5 and they attack x3
once you fill that up to 5/5, the next tentacle gain does nothing. the second tentacle gain increases it to 5/6 and they attack x3
once you fill that up to 6/6, the third tentacle gain makes it 6/7, etc

yeah, that kind of expending progression feels more like what is going on. The tooltip, at least in English, talks about anything else but that lol. I guess t just omits the fact that a tentacle needs to be created and that is what increases 1 by 1 too
yeah when it expands it doesn't fill the slot instantly, and the more you expand the more tentacles you need over the limit to expand further
the tooltip should have a "instead" to show you don't gain+expand in the same turn, 2nd part does explain you need an additional trigger everytime
tentacle count and tentacle limit are two different things
that might be what's causing the confusion
I thought the count is how many you actually have. So if you have 4 and the limit is 4, then creating 5th makes the count exceed the limit and that counts as a 'trigger'.
that's correct, but the trigger doesn't actually make one
because you're already at limit
Wait so what happens when you are limit

but they say 'when the count exceeds the limit' lol, that's the wording issue I guess cause it implies going above the limit. They really can have one English speaking guy hired to reword these 2 sentences lol... but I guess since everyone knows eventually how it works, it's no big deal

'if you are at a limit and attempt to create a tentacle, then a new empty tentacle slot is created instead. For each tentacle slot created this way in a battle one extra tentacle creation is needed to create the next slot' - something like that lol
ok so I think myriam rouse can create a tentacle and a slot at the same time so it’s twice the speed?
yeah, some abilities increase the limit directly

<@376592242355470338> E2 GMurphy + her wheel, Thais E2 + her wheel.
Within those you can get GMurphy to E3 depending on how actively you will play for the next 2 weeks.
does e2 mean 2 copies

E0 is one copy, basically unlocking an awakener.
E2 is 2 copies after that, totaling in 3 copies.

E refers to Enlightenments. E0 = no Enligtenments unlocked.
E1 - one, E2 - two, E3 - 3 Enlightenments.
what order should i got for unlocking the copies and wheels

An awakener to a desired Enlightenment first, then a wheel.
I would prioritise GMurphy first since her banner expires sooner.
But like I said, if you play actively within next 2 weeks, you will be able to get GMurphy E3 + her wheel, Thais E2 + her wheel.
depends
id say get thais wheel before getting e2 thais
If there's a situation where you can't play due to irl stuff, you can get a copy of awakener and then a copy of a wheel. In the worst case, you will dupe that with monthly items.

skills can consume rituals to do better effects. when consumed, they change to sacrement and stay in your hand, removing some of your draws next turn. If they're in your hand at the start of the turn they change back to ritual so you can reuse them every turn. Don't keep more than 3, in fact it's also fine to just use them to feed Myriam aliemus and just somewhat ignore the mech for handspace, depends on the situation
so gain ritual>use ritual> keep sacrement until 3 turn then use it or use it as a battery like that ?

I meant don't keep more than 3 of these cards in hand cause the bonus for ritual caps out at 3 rituals so have like 5 in hand is just a waste
but even 3 isn't necessarily worth the handspace/draw, depends on the situation

hp scales right?

the actual scaling is a bit wonky but it's more than linear
keeper level to HP/CON goes something like
Keeper Level 2 - 0.49HP/CON
Keeper Level 49 - 1.5HP/CON
Keeper Level 70 - 2.6HP/CON
it's a bit more difficult to plot the data for this now that the text for HP/CON scaling has been removed from the level up pop up
similar behavior can be seen with Relics, Posses, and Orisons

as for tentacle damage, or shields, those scale off of other factors but keeper level may affect them as well though indirectly
